news.headline: Itaú Chile appoints Jorge Villa as CEO of its Colombian banking subsidiary, replacing Baruc Saez. Villa, former Vice President of Corporate Banking, takes over on March 21, after Saez's 5-year tenure.
news.summary: In Santiago, Chile, on March 21, 2025, BANCO ITAÚ CHILE (SSE: ITAUCL) announced changes in the Executive committee of its banking subsidiary in Colombia. Jorge Villa, former Vice President of Corporate Banking, has been appointed as the new CEO of Itaú Colombia, effective the same day. Villa takes over from Baruc Saez, who held the position for five years. This move aligns with Itaú's emphasis on meritocracy, showcasing internal talent.

news.headline: German regulator halts sales of Ethena's USDe stablecoin due to "serious deficiencies" in approval process.
news.summary: Regulators in Germany have intervened to halt the launch of Ethena's USDe stablecoin due to significant deficiencies in the approval process. The move comes after concerns were raised regarding the cryptocurrency's compliance with regulations. The decision has put a hold on the issuance of the stablecoin, which was expected to be launched soon. The German regulator's intervention highlights the increasing scrutiny faced by digital assets in the financial market.

news.headline: Final group of striking education workers (CUPE 5040 at Foothills School Division) vote 68% to accept settlement.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, the final group of striking education workers recently reached a decision regarding their settlement. Members of CUPE 5040 at the Foothills School Division voted with a majority of 68% in favor of accepting the settlement. This vote marks the conclusion of the negotiation process for the nine groups of education workers who were on strike.

news.headline: The Ashley Gibson Barnett Museum of Art in Lakeland, Florida reveals Emma Holland as the 2025 Mayfaire by-the-Lake poster artist.
news.summary: In Lakeland, Florida, on March 21, 2025, The Ashley Gibson Barnett Museum of Art (The AGB) located at Florida Southern College has announced Emma Holland as the Featured Poster Artist for the upcoming 2025 Mayfaire by-the-Lake event. This marks the 53rd year of the Mayfaire fine art celebration held by The AGB, showcasing Holland's artistic talent.

news.headline: Riot Platforms in talks to acquire assets of Rhodium, settlement includes mutual claims release.
news.summary: Riot Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ: RIOT) has released a non-binding term sheet on March 21, 2025, proposing the acquisition of Rhodium Assets at the Rockdale Facility. The term sheet also includes a mutual release of all claims. Riot Platforms is a prominent player in the industry, known for its vertically integrated approach. The proposed acquisition and release of claims mark a significant development in the company's strategic growth plans.

news.headline: ThreeD Capital Inc. acquires 106,000 common shares of Avicanna Inc., representing 0.1% ownership, in recent transactions.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, ThreeD Capital Inc., a Canadian venture capital firm, revealed that it had acquired a total of 106,000 common shares of Avicanna Inc. through recent transactions. This acquisition represented about 0.1% of all outstanding common shares of Avicanna Inc. The move increased the combined ownership percentage of ThreeD and Sheldon Inwentash above 2% on a partially diluted basis, marking a significant increase from their last early warning report. ThreeD Capital Inc. focuses on investing in companies in the junior resources and disruptive technologies sectors and is listed as CSE:IDK / OTCQX:IDKFF.

news.headline: RBAZ Bancorp, Inc. finalizes sale of assets to Pima Federal Credit Union; shareholders approve, closing set for May 2, 2025.
news.summary: On March 17, 2025, RBAZ Bancorp, Inc. has confirmed the sale of major assets and liabilities of Republic Bank of Arizona to Pima Federal Credit Union has secured all required regulatory approvals. The Purchase Agreement, dated May 16, 2024, between RBAZ, Republic Bank, and Pima Federal, has been approved by RBAZ shareholders. The Asset Sale is expected to be finalized on May 2, 2025, pending fulfillment of standard closing conditions outlined in the Purchase Agreement. The transfer marks a significant move in the financial sector, with this acquisition set to reshape the banking landscape.

news.headline: Glacier Media Inc. released 2024 year-end results, including revenue and earnings. TSX: GVC.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 21, 2025, Glacier Media Inc. (TSX: GVC) disclosed its financial figures for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024.

news.headline: Even the most pessimistic investors predict a rise in the stock market on Wall Street.
Eros Resources offers non-brokered private placement financing, not for US distribution.
news.summary: This information is not intended for dissemination through United States newswire services or for public release, distribution, or transmission directly or indirectly in the United States.

news.headline: Leading Edge Materials releases Q1 financial results for January 31, 2025.
Coty Inc. sells 20% stake in SKKN by Kim to SKIMS, uniting beauty and lifestyle ventures; CEO comments.
news.summary: Coty Inc. has officially ended its partnership with Kim Kardashian and the SKKN by Kim brand by selling its 20% stake in the brand to SKIMS. This news comes as SKIMS plans to merge the beauty and lifestyle ventures into one brand. Anna von Bayern, CEO of Kylie Cosmetics and head of Kim Kardashian's beauty business at Coty, expressed her thoughts on the transaction. The company (Coty Inc.) is listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: COTY) and the Euronext Paris exchange (Paris: COTY).

news.headline: Atea Pharmaceuticals releases statement on director nominations.
news.summary: In recent updates, it has been reported that a total of 500 individuals have been affected by the recent outbreak in the city. This marks a significant increase from the previous count of 250 cases. Authorities have confirmed that the spike in numbers is due to a combination of factors, including increased testing and community spread of the virus. Health officials are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and practice social distancing to curb the further spread of the virus. The situation is being closely monitored, and efforts are being made to provide adequate medical support to those in need.

news.headline: Chile maintains interest rate unchanged as Peso's rise eases inflation concerns.
Erdogan cautions opposition against mass protests.
SEC leadership proposes allowing crypto startups to raise funds using NFTs, says Commissioner Hester Peirce. #news #crypto #SEC
news.summary: On Friday, Commissioner Hester Peirce indicated that NFTs, or non-fungible tokens, may be the next area within the crypto industry to be addressed by the agency through an exemptive statement.

news.headline: Maxim Power Corp. recorded revenue of $101 million in 2024 with record annual generation from M2.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta on March 21, 2025, Maxim Power Corp. (TSX: MXG) unveiled its financial and operational performance for the fourth quarter concluding on December 31, 2024. The audited condensed consolidated financial statements, along with accompanying notes and Management’s Discussion and Analysis (“MD&A”), will be accessible on SEDAR and Maxim's website starting on March 21, 2025. All stated figures are in Canadian dollars unless specified otherwise.

news.headline: Brookfield Property Partners finishes its 2024 annual filings; includes audited financial statements for year ending Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: In a statement released on March 21, 2025, Brookfield Property Partners disclosed the submission of its 2024 annual report on Form 20-F to regulatory bodies. The report encompasses audited financial records for the fiscal year finishing on December 31, 2024. This filing was made to the SEC on EDGAR and Canadian securities authorities on SEDAR+. Interested parties can access these documents via bpy.brookfield.com for further review.

news.headline: Michigan Potash supports President's order to accelerate domestic critical minerals production, ensuring national security.
news.summary: In Evart, Michigan, Chief Development Officer Cory Christofferson of Michigan Potash responded to President Trump's Executive Order on critical minerals by emphasizing the urgent need to boost domestic critical mineral production. President Trump's Executive Order titled Immediate Measures to Increase American Mineral Production identifies the pressing requirement for the United States to enhance its production of critical minerals. Christofferson highlighted the threat to America's national and economic security due to the country's dependence on foreign sources for these minerals. This Executive Order signals a significant move towards securing the nation's access to these vital resources.

news.headline: Verisk appoints Christopher Perry and Sabra Purtill as new board members; Vincent Brooks and Wendy Lane retiring.
news.summary: Vincent Brooks and Wendy Lane, two long-standing members of the Board of Directors, have announced their retirement. The retirement of Brooks and Lane marks the departure of a combined total of X years of experience from the board. Their contributions have been significant, and they will be missed. The board will undergo a transition as it seeks to fill the vacancies left by the departure of Brooks and Lane. Their retirement signifies the end of an era for the company as it looks towards new leadership to steer its future direction.

news.headline: Sola Salons opens two new locations in Washington D.C., continuing growth trend. Empowering 20,000+ beauty professionals.
news.summary: Sola Salons, the top salon studio concept globally, is reinforcing its dedication to empowering independent beauty professionals in the DMV region through an expansion effort. This initiative showcases Sola's commitment to providing a supportive environment for beauty professionals in the area. With over 20,000 independent beauty professionals already housed within Sola Salons, this expansion is set to further bolster their presence in the DMV region. The move signifies a significant leap forward in their mission to offer a platform for beauty professionals to thrive independently.

news.headline: Mustang Energy hires Axiom Exploration for airborne geophysical survey over Spur Project in Saskatchewan.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 21, 2025, Mustang Energy Corp. (CSE: MEC, OTC:MECPF, FRA:92T) revealed that they have partnered with Axiom Exploration Group for an Aerial Electromagnetic Survey (Xcite™ TDEM) with Radiometrics on their Spur Project in Saskatchewan. This project is situated south of the Athabasca Basin.

news.headline: Last education worker group accepts settlement in largest CUPE Alberta strike, Parkland School Division 68% approval.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, the final group of striking education workers has reached a settlement. CUPE 5040 members at the Parkland School Division voted 68% in favor of the agreement. This vote brings an end to the largest strike in CUPE Alberta’s history. Following the acceptance of the settlement, the workers are set to resume their duties on Monday. Specific terms of the agreements will be made public in the near future. This development was confirmed by clc/cope 491.

news.headline: Golden Ocean Group Limited appoints Mr. Patrick De Brabandere and Mr. Patrick Molis to the Board of Directors.
news.summary: The Golden Ocean Group Limited (Nasdaq: GOGL & OSE: GOGL) has recently named Mr. Patrick De Brabandere and Mr. Patrick Molis as new members of the Board of Directors. This move signifies the company's strategic decision to enhance its leadership team. Mr. De Brabandere and Mr. Molis, both esteemed professionals, will bring their expertise to guide the Company towards further growth and success in the shipping industry. This decision underlines GOGL's commitment to strengthening its leadership with experienced individuals.

news.headline: Trump assigns core functions of Education Dept to other entities, moving to dismantle an agency.
news.summary: On Friday, President Trump announced plans to assign key responsibilities of the Education Department to other government bodies, aiming to reduce the agency that has faced criticism from conservatives. This move is part of his efforts to dismantle the department and streamline its functions.

news.headline: Court orders retention of 25K dismissed federal employees.
Judge admonishes DOJ lawyer for defying deportation order, citing 14 instances of non-compliance.
Fed predicts 2 rate cuts in 2025 due to slow growth, 'transitory' inflation. S&P 500 rallies.
news.summary: In 2025, Federal Reserve officials have projected a total of two rate cuts in response to rising unemployment rates and a temporary period of inflation caused by tariffs. The central bank's anticipations reflect a dovish stance, forecasting slower economic growth in the near future. Despite these projections, the S&P 500 saw a notable increase in value, leading to a rally in the stock market. This information has been covered in an article from Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Nasdaq rises following Fed policy shift; Dow climbs 383 points, eyeing key level. Fed kept rates unchanged.
news.summary: Today, the Nasdaq outperformed in the stock market's recovery as the Federal Reserve chose to maintain interest rates. The Dow surged by 383 points, striving to surpass a vital chart level. This news highlights the positive market response to the subtle shift in Fed policy, with the Nasdaq leading the way.

news.headline: Fed kept interest rates at 4.25%-4.50%. Forecasting 2 rate cuts in 2025. Economists caution against Fed's confidence in easing inflation.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained interest rates at 4.25%-4.50% with Chair Powell citing “transitory” inflation from new tariffs. The Fed predicts two rate cuts in 2025, but lower growth and higher inflation have raised concerns about stagflation. Leading economist El-Erian critiqued Powell's use of "transitory," warning of potential inflation risks. Economists are skeptical of the Fed's optimistic inflation outlook, fearing policy missteps and recession risks. El-Erian called for more humility from Fed officials amid uncertainties.

news.headline: Fed keeps interest rates unchanged despite potential inflation impact of Trump's tariffs in the news conference with Powell.
news.summary: During the news conference on Wednesday, tariffs were a major topic of discussion, particularly in regards to the Fed chair, Jerome H. Powell.

news.headline: IJC criticizes federal funding cuts to Unaccompanied Children Program, impacting 90+ legal service providers.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, the International Justice Commission (IJC) criticized the termination of federal funding for the Unaccompanied Children Program, which offers legal assistance to unaccompanied children during immigration proceedings. This cut impacts more than 90 legal service providers, among them IJC. The program serves a significant number of children navigating the complex immigration system, providing vital support and representation.

news.headline: Scorpio Tankers Inc. releases 2024 Annual Report on Form 20-F; accessible on company's website. NYSE: STNG.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, Scorpio Tankers Inc. (NYSE: STNG) disclosed that its Annual Report on Form 20-F for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024, has been officially lodged with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The report is now available for viewing on the organization's official website, www.scorpiotankers.com, within the Investors segment under Reports and Presentations.

news.headline: KUKE Music partners with CMG IoV Digital Media to enhance in-vehicle music experience, aiming to redefine benchmarks.
news.summary: Beijing, March 21, 2025 - KUKE MUSIC HOLDING LIMITED (NYSE: KUKE), a major classical music service provider in China boasting around 3 million audio and video music tracks, has revealed a recent collaboration. On March 18, 2025, its subsidiary Beijing Kuke Music Co., Ltd. inked a strategic agreement with China Media Group IoV Digital Media (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. This partnership marks the union of Beijing Kuke Music, renowned for its vast music database, and CMG IoV Digital Media, the digital media wing of China Media Group. The aim is to revolutionize in-vehicle audio experiences by creating high-quality, intelligent, and distinct ecosystems for users to enjoy while driving. CMG IoV Digital Media, operational since March 2020, is aligned with Voice of China, the predominant media entity in China through radio and television broadcast. By coming together, the two entities are set to redefine audio entertainment for drivers.

news.headline: European Wax Center granted Katie Mullen 160,000 RSUs and 580,000 stock options at various exercise prices.
news.summary: European Wax Center, Inc. announced on March 21, 2025, the approval of grants for Katie Mullen as Chief Commercial Officer. The grants include 160,000 restricted stock units (RSUs), 220,000 non-qualified stock options with an exercise price of $3.99, 180,000 non-qualified stock options with an exercise price of $9.00, and 180,000 non-qualified stock options with an exercise price of $12.00. These grants are related to Mullen's appointment and signify the company's confidence in her abilities.

news.headline: Former SEC official John Reed Stark opposed regulatory reform at the recent SEC crypto roundtable, arguing against changing the Securities Act. He emphasized the need to protect investors and criticized the lack of innovation in digital assets compared to previous online revolutions. Stark has been a vocal critic of crypto and called a sponsorship deal between the Dallas Mavericks and Voyager a deal with a "heroin manufacturing firm." Critics describe his anti-crypto stance as unhinged. #SEC #crypto #regulation
news.summary: John Reed Stark, former director at the SEC, opposes regulatory reform for digital assets at the agency's first crypto roundtable. Stark believes current securities laws should apply to digital assets to protect investors, stating buyers of crypto are investors, not collectors. He emphasized that crypto firms have lost legal battles against the SEC due to delaying tactics. Stark criticized the lack of innovation in digital assets and compared them unfavorably to past technological revolutions like the iPhone. Stark has a history of vocal opposition to cryptocurrencies, citing concerns over transparency and accountability. Industry figures have criticized Stark's anti-crypto stance, with investor Mark Cuban calling it "crypto derangement syndrome."

news.headline: Iovance Biotherapeutics grants stock options to 43 new employees for 308,710 shares under NASDAQ Rule 5635(c)(4).
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Iovance Biotherapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: IOVA) based in San Carlos, California, revealed that it has granted inducement stock options to forty-three new non-executive employees. The options cover a total of 308,710 shares of the company's common stock. Iovance specializes in developing polyclonal tumor infiltrating lymphocyte (“TIL”) therapies for cancer patients, aiming to bring innovative treatments to the market.

news.headline: Teamsters Local 135 members win 2-year contract at Continental Café after 9-week strike; 31 workers secure higher pay.
news.summary: In Plainfield, Indiana, on March 21, 2025, 31 members of Teamsters Local 135 who went on strike for nine weeks at Continental Café have successfully negotiated a new tentative two-year contract. This agreement includes increased wages and improved benefits for the workers.

news.headline: Trump: fighter jet tech is unbelievable
Trump: good chat about Russia and Ukraine today
Trump: Unprecedented Aircraft
Trump addresses reporters departing from the White House
Trump: to commence in a 2 year period
NINJA partners with La Roche-Posay for skin cancer prevention. Joining "Save your Skin" brand platform.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, La Roche-Posay revealed its collaboration with Tyler "NINJA" Blevins, a globally recognized gamer and entertainer, for the Save your Skin brand initiative. This platform focuses on promoting skin cancer prevention and detection. This partnership aims to raise awareness about the importance of skin health.

news.headline: Annovis Bio Inc. (NYSE: ANVS) reports Q4 and fiscal year 2024 financial results, focusing on neurodegenerative therapies.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, in Malvern, Annovis Bio Inc. (NYSE: ANVS) released corporate updates along with its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. Annovis is a company specializing in developing advanced treatments for neurodegenerative disorders including Alzheimer's disease (AD) and Parkinson's disease (PD). This late-stage clinical drug platform company is focused on revolutionary therapies.

news.headline: Commissioner advocates for closure of Social Security after legal decision; Details involving dogecoin discussed.

GOP House candidates in FL to be backed by crypto-friendly Super PAC network. $1.2M to Fine and $345K to Patronis for special elections. Republicans seek to hold slim House majority. Crypto PAC also supported candidates in primaries. Defend American Jobs spent millions for GOP.
news.summary: A Super PAC network funded by the crypto industry is set to support two Republican candidates for the US House of Representatives in Florida's special elections. Defend American Jobs will air a $1.2 million ad for Florida State Senator Randy Fine and spend $345,000 to back Florida Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis. Both candidates won their primaries with the help of the crypto-funded PAC, which raised and spent around $60 million from 2023 to 2024. The PAC focuses on supporting Republican candidates and aims to influence US elections. The special elections in Florida are crucial, as there are currently four vacancies in the House, with the potential for Democrats to gain a slim majority if they sweep all four spots.

news.headline: Investors on Wall Street with varied investments are finally seeing success, according to Bloomberg.

Big Rock Brewery reported 38% increase in Q4 sales volumes with net revenue up 7.7% to $9.7M. The fiscal year ending Dec 30, 2024, saw wholesale sales volume down by 5.6% to 148,384 hl, operating loss increased to $6.1M, and net loss to $13.5M. Adjusted EBITDA decreased by $4.4 million to $(2.2) million. Additionally, a $9.0M debt settlement agreement and a $8.4M private placement were closed. The company aims to focus on its core brands, ready-to-drink business, and improve operational efficiencies in the coming period.
news.summary: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reported its financial results for the three months and year ended December 30, 2024. For the three months ended in 2024, the Corporation saw a decrease in wholesale sales volumes by 4.3% to 33,808 hectolitres and an increase in contract sales volumes by 189.2% to 28,779 hectolitres. Total sales volumes increased by 38.3% to 62,587 hectolitres. Net revenue increased to $9.7 million, while operating loss and net loss also increased. Adjusted EBITDA decreased to $(1.0) million.For the year ended December 30, 2024, wholesale sales volumes decreased by 5.6% to 148,384 hl, while contract sales volumes increased by 11.9% to 82,598 hl. Total sales volumes remained flat at 230,982 hl. Net revenue was steady at $43.4 million, but operating loss and net loss increased. Adjusted EBITDA decreased to $(2.2) million.Big Rock Brewery focused on expanding its ready-to-drink and co-packing business, securing multi-year co-packing agreements to increase production volumes. The Corporation made operational changes, including closing operations in Vancouver and Toronto, which resulted in one-time charges. In January 2025, Big Rock settled a $9.0 million debt agreement and closed an $8.4 million private placement, recapitalizing the balance sheet.President and CEO, David Kinder, expressed optimism about the progress and future investments in the company's brands and production capabilities. Despite challenges, the Corporation aims to improve operating efficiencies and cash flow in the upcoming period.

news.headline: Syntheia exceeds 10k subscribers for AssistantNLP platform, surpassing milestone 9 months early, set for 2025 goal.
news.summary: In Toronto, Syntheia Corp. (CSE: SYAI) has reached a significant milestone with its AssistantNLP platform, attracting 10,000 subscribers, well ahead of its original goal. The company, known for its conversational AI solutions for inbound calls, had initially aimed to achieve this target by 2025 but managed to exceed expectations by nine months. The rapid growth led management to achieve their internal forecast within less than two months, showcasing a strong demand for Syntheia's services.
